mission.title: knative: Allow runtimeClassName and dnsPolicy in service template spec
mission.description: Allow runtimeClassName and dnsPolicy in service template spec. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current knative deployment
steps.description:
Verify your knative version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative
helm list -n knative 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working knative installation.

steps.title: Review knative configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant knative configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative
kubectl get configmap -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=knative
```
## In what area(s)?

## Describe the feature

Allow `runtimeClassName` and `dnsPolicy` fields in the service template spec (currently disallowed

steps.title: Apply the fix for Allow runtimeClassName and dnsPolicy in service template…
steps.description:
Allow users to set runtimeClassName on services via the runtimeclassname
feature flag.

Issue #5306

**Release Note**
```yaml
spec:
  runtimeClassName: 'kata-qemu' # or 'runsc' (gvisor)
  dnsPolicy: 'Default'
  ...
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n knative -l app.kubernetes.io/name=knative
kubectl get events -n knative --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Allow runtimeClassName and dnsPolicy in service template…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Allow users to set runtimeClassName on services via the runtimeclassname
feature flag.

Issue #5306

**Release Note**

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with knative installed or the issue environment reproducible.
